Health Milestones
We respectfully acknowledge The Wollithiga and Yorta Yorta Peoples as the traditional custodians of the land of the communities that the Njernda Aboriginal Corporation service.
The 5 Ds of Quitting

Feeling a craving right now? Tap one and do it immediately.

⏱️
D
DELAY
Wait 10 minutes — cravings pass on their own
🏃
D
DISTRACT
Move your body or change what you are doing
🌬️
D
DEEP BREATHE
In 4, hold 4, out 4. Repeat four times
💧
D
DRINK WATER
Sip slowly — keeps hands busy, body hydrated
🤝
D
DISCUSS
Talk to family, Mob, or Uncle Keith
Appointment 1 — Smoking Diary

For the first 7 days record every cigarette you smoke — when, where, and what caused it. No judgment — just information. By Appointment 2 your triggers will be clear and we can work on them together.

STEP 1 OF 2
Your History
Background — Tell Us About You

These questions help Uncle Keith understand your smoking history and what will work best for you. Complete this once before starting your daily diary.

1. How long have you been involved with smoking?
2. Have you ever tried to become smokefree before?
3. What did you find most difficult when you tried or thought about quitting?
4. Tell us something positive you have experienced when smoking
5. Tell us something negative you have experienced when smoking
6. Do you get upset or angry when you cannot smoke when you want to?
If yes or somewhat — describe one time this happened
STEP 2 OF 2
Where to Now
Where to Now

These questions help us plan your smokefree journey together.

What are your thoughts on what we have gathered so far?
Do you want to look at ways to becoming smokefree?
What strategies do you think would work best for you?
Why do you believe this would be your best option?
What areas of your health — mental, physical, and emotional wellbeing — would you like to work on that would best help you on your smokefree path?
Are you truly ready to make the first step to becoming smokefree?
✓ Background Saved

This will be reviewed with Uncle Keith at Appointment 1. You can scroll down to start recording your daily cigarettes.

Record Each Cigarette
Time smoked
Where were you?
What caused it? (the trigger)
How were you feeling?
How strong was the urge? (1–5)
Any other notes?

This summary will be reviewed with Uncle Keith at Appointment 2.

Record each cigarette here. No judgment — just information.

Appointment 2 — Craving Diary

Your triggers have been identified. Now we track cravings and how you managed them. Use the 5 Ds and record what worked. Every entry is progress.

Your craving entries will appear here.

Week 3 — NRT Monitoring

You have been issued 7 days of patches plus either gum or spray. Record your patch each day and every time you use your gum or spray. This helps Uncle Keith monitor your progress and make sure you are using NRT safely — patches and top-up NRT together must be carefully tracked to avoid overuse.

Your NRT Preference

Select which top-up NRT you were issued alongside your patches.

Daily Patch Record

Record your patch each day — when you put it on and when you removed it.

Date
Patch strength
Time patch was placed on

Patch location on body

How did you feel today overall?

Top-Up NRT Usage
⚠️ You have used your top-up NRT multiple times today while wearing a patch. Please contact Uncle Keith if cravings are this frequent — your plan may need adjusting.

Record each time you use your gum or spray while wearing the patch. This should only be used when a craving breaks through — not as a routine.

Daily Check-In

Take a moment each day to reflect on how you are going. This is your record — honest and private.

How are you feeling today overall?
Did you use your patch today?

🏆
PROGRAM COMPLETE!
You have completed the 28-day Yorta Ngata Dhonga Mathimna program. This is an extraordinary achievement. Your journey to a healthier, smokefree life has truly begun.
Contact Uncle Keith to discuss your next steps and ongoing support.
Your 4 Appointments

Each appointment builds on the last over four consecutive weeks. Complete all four for the best results.

1 ✓ Completed
Your Starting Point
Your smoking history was discussed and you were issued a Diary to record every cigarette over 7 days — noting the time, location, cause, and how you were feeling. These entries identify your key triggers for Appointment 2.
2 📍 Current Focus
Understanding Your Triggers
Your Week 1 diary is reviewed together. Triggers, needs, and habits are discussed and strategies put in place. Treatment options are explored. A new craving diary is issued — now tracking how you manage cravings using the 5 Ds.
3 Upcoming
Making Your Decision
We reflect on your progress and the positives. Decisions are made together about where your personal journey will now take you. Seven days of your chosen therapy begins, monitored daily via text or phone.
4 Upcoming
Your New Beginning
We reflect on all three sessions and confirm the best options for becoming fully smokefree. Already you question yourself every time you smoke — that is a big step. Your healthier life has already begun.

Need Support Right Now?

Uncle Keith is here to help. Do not wait for your next appointment.

🔴 Quitline 13 7848
About the Program

Yorta Ngata Dhonga Mathimna — I say NO to smoking — a culturally grounded 28-day smokefree program from Njernda Aboriginal Corporation. Built on relationship, respect, and genuine belief in you.

10 Reasons to Quit Beyond the Big Health Threats
01
Enhanced hygiene
02
Boost taste & smell
03
Improved appearance
04
Gain freedom
05
Positive role model
06
More fitness & energy
07
Reduce environmental impact
08
Protect loved ones
09
Improved social life
10
Save money for what matters
Treatment Options
Patches, lozenges, gum, and mouth spray — supported and monitored through Njernda. Ask Uncle Keith about the right option for you.
Available on the PBS at a reduced cost. Your Njernda GP will arrange an appointment and monitor your progress.
Free, confidential telephone coaching available any time. Njernda will assist with your referral, or you can call directly right now.
Referral and ongoing support arranged throughout your program as needed. Speak to Uncle Keith about what feels right for you.
Every Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander person can access a free yearly 715 Health Assessment at Njernda. Completed at the start of the program to gauge your current health. Call (03) 5481 0600 to book.

Njernda Aboriginal Corporation

I say NO to smoking
Acknowledgement of Country

We respectfully acknowledge the Wollithiga and Yorta Yorta Peoples as the traditional custodians of the land and waterways of the communities that the Njernda Aboriginal Corporation serves.

We pay our deepest respects to Elders past, present, and emerging. We recognise their enduring connection to Country, their strength, their wisdom, and their ongoing contribution to the health and wellbeing of community.

This program was developed on Yorta Yorta Country, for Yorta Yorta and broader Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander community, with deep respect for culture, language, and the healing power of Country.

The Name — What It Means
YORTA NGATA
Our people, our Country, our identity
DHONGA MATHIMNA
I say NO to smoking

The name of this program carries Yorta Yorta language — a living language, rooted in this Country. By naming this program in language, we honour the strength of our culture and affirm that healing is not separate from identity. Yorta Ngata Dhonga Mathimna — our people say no to smoking.

Cultural Safety at the Heart

This program is built on the understanding that healing happens in relationship. The Yorta Ngata Dhonga Mathimna program is grounded in:

🤝
Relationship first
Every person is met with respect, dignity, and without judgment
🌿
Country as healer
Connection to Country, Mob, and culture strengthens the journey
💪
Strengths-based
We build on what you already have — your resilience, your community, your story
❤️
No shame
Quitting is hard. Slipping does not mean failing. We walk this together
About Njernda Aboriginal Corporation

Njernda Aboriginal Corporation is a community-controlled health organisation serving Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people in Echuca and the surrounding region. Njernda means home in Yorta Yorta language.

The Healthy Lifestyle program supports community members to live well — through smokefree programs, nutrition and physical activity support, health promotion, and one-on-one coaching with Uncle Keith Hearn.

CONTACT
